back_translation: When the Sabbath day had passed, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought aromatic spices in order to go and anoint the body.
Very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they came to the tomb.
They were saying among themselves: 'Who will roll the stone away from the entrance of the tomb for us?'
When they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away, even though the stone was very large.
When they entered the tomb, they saw a young man wearing a long white robe, sitting on the right side, and they were astonished.
But he said to them: 'Do not be astonished. You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth who was crucified on the cross. He has risen; He is not here. Look at the place where they laid Him.
But go and tell His disciples and Peter: He is going ahead of you into the region of Galilee; there you will see Him, as He told you.'

They went out from the tomb and ran away, for trembling and astonishment had overcome them. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. ⟦‹Shorter ending› Then they reported all that had been commanded briefly to those around Peter. After that, Jesus Himself sent forth through them, from east to west, the holy and incorruptible proclamation of eternal salvation. Amen.⟧
⟦When He had risen early on the first day of the week, He appeared first to Mary Magdalene, the woman from whom He had cast out seven demons.
⟦She went and told those who had been with Him, who were mourning and weeping.
⟦But when they heard that He was alive and that Mary had seen Him, they did not believe.
⟦After that, He appeared in a different form to two of them while they were walking out into the countryside.
⟦They went back and told the rest, but they did not believe them either.

⟦Afterward, He appeared to the eleven while they were reclining at table, and rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they had not believed those who had seen Him after He had risen.
⟦And He said to them: 'Go out into all the world, proclaim the gospel to all of creation.
⟦Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.
⟦And these signs will follow those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons, they will speak in new tongues,
⟦they will pick up serpents; if they drink anything deadly, it will not harm them in any way. They will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.'
⟦After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, He was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
⟦As for them, they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked together with them, and confirmed the word through the signs that followed.⟧
